title: cladobium longicolle
side_effects: Cladobium longicolle can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds like saponins and anthraquinones. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to kidney damage and an increased risk of bleeding, as it may interfere with blood clotting factors. In severe cases, improper use of Cladobium longicolle can cause liver damage, potentially resulting in liver failure. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using the plant altogether, as its toxic compounds can be transmitted to the fetus or baby.
